So we are a Y Combinator X startup.

We basically, our product is pretty niche.
So basically, we help import exporters get tariff refunds whenever they import, they pay tariffs, duties, and U.S.
government has a program called Duty Drawback that enable these companies to get a refund out of those duty and fees paid if they later on have eligible activities, such as manufacturing the U.S.
or export from the U.S., destruction of the U.S., so on and so forth, to do this end-to-end with AI.
So we are a full service provider, but we are hiring our services with AI, so that's more cost-efficient, time-efficient.

We just started on Google Ads.

I'm curious, in this space, like, you know, understand there's an AI solution to this.
Is there a conventional, like, human-led approach you're competing with?
How much solution awareness is there in the market to this?
Yeah, so I guess traditionally, it's mostly, there's, like, duty drawbacks to this character that only do duty drawback.
Mm-hmm.
Mm Mm Mm Mm-hmm.
There's also like free-for-orders, service providers, other service providers, free-for-orders, custom brokers that also offer eGrowback as a service.
Typically, it takes them 6 to 12 months to process a claim because they're doing this manually or with Excel sheet.
And so we want to build sort of a turbo-tax-like experience where all the customers need to do is upload their data into our platform and we'll handle everything automatically starting from the data.
So, yeah, I think it's a very different approach and the efficiency and the costs that we offer, it's really sort of revolutionizing the industry.
But I think the problem here is like, first of all, not everyone knows about drawback, even though they're eligible.
So there's like an education piece that needs to go on.
And then secondly is how do we position ourselves clearly on the website, on the Internet, such that people do see the difference between us and the traditional service provider.

So when you have low authority, can be really hard to rank for difficult keywords.

That's usually not how it goes at this phase.

What does that mean to you at this point?

think a few things that sort of are more measurable are like the rankings for the keywords.

While consistently hitting a high bar of quality content, because that is one of the challenges with AI.

can do okay at that, but oftentimes there's key patterns and just things that's missing.

And where do we bring humans in the loop throughout the entire process?
And so what we found is, while you can create the most amazing set of workflows, you can in the world continue to refine that.

If you don't have the right inputs, the right strategy that goes into them, if you don't have someone reviewing those outputs and tweaking and continuing to refine, you're not going to get to some repeatable level of quality that's going to move the needle.
And so a lot of our process and approaches is with that in mind.

Perspective of what it is we do.

You know, a bit of where this all started for us is, you know, we've all been, we are a form of a group of many different in-house marketers who have built content teams and found a lot of success in doing that.

But there's a lot of challenges in coordinating those teams together in terms of, like, building that right team, finding the right topics, figuring out the right editorial layer for repeatable, high-quality content.
Usually there's important friction points around, you know, creating of things like images or getting that content live and staged and published.
And so a lot of what we do is really with trying to own that end-to-end for our customers and laying that strategic foundation from a content strategy standpoint.
To help in creation of the content, help create the images, restage and publish the content for the vast majority of our accounts.
And with that in mind, that is where a lot of our initial engagements start, is building out what we kind of call the editorial engine.
Editorial meaning longer form content.
This could be blogs, but certainly does not have to be limited to blogs.
There can be other scenarios where longer form content can sit in the other part of your website that is a unique experience we could create tailored for your customers.
But the reason we start these engagements here is we just find a lot of benefit of getting really deep on your personas, the right tone and voice and styles to apply.
Starting to go deep in terms of the topics and how are those topics resonating with your personas, what is and what is not resonating.
And all that is really, really rich, important signal for us to not only continue to grow.
Editorial engine and find opportunities to drive more traffic and conversions through that alone.

So that could mean going after varying programmatic opportunities, programmatic meaning like template-based pages typically that you can scale up really, really fast.
For early domains like yourself, we don't typically recommend starting there.

We typically recommend if you have opportunities here to build topical and domain authority before really scaling out your content to a high level, just to like make sure you have a healthy foundation for your website.
